description.undefined: Ontology for representing medical devices, manufacturers, incidents, risks and corrective actions in regulatory surveillance scenarios.

labels.en: Medical Devices Classification and Safety Ontology

comments.undefined: Ontology about medical devices, their classification, incidents, risks and corrective actions.

isDefinedBy.value:
The authors of this ontology have been:

Diana Alejandrina Socola

Tomás Cuevas

includedInDataCatalog.value: This ontology is about medical devices, their classification, and the safety mechanisms associated with their use, including incidents and corrective actions.

abstract.value: This ontology provides a semantic model for high-risk medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices, including their manufacturers, risk classes, incidents, and field safety corrective actions. It supports formal knowledge representation, reasoning, and interoperability in regulatory surveillance scenarios under MDR and IVDR frameworks.

comment.en: Field safety corrective action consisting in the removal of a medical device from the market or from use due to safety or performance concerns.
The natural or legal person who manufactures or fully refurbishes a device or has a device designed, manufactured or fully refurbished, and markets that device under its name or trademark.
Represents the type of medical device.
represents the Medical Device Regulation applicable in the European Union
Represents a high probability level for a medical device risk
Risk of harm due to unauthorized access, interference, or data breach affecting the safety or performance of a medical device.
Extent to which use of the device does not expose patients, users or others to unacceptable risk under normal conditions of use
Represents an active implant medical device type.
represents a risk class C for in vitro diagnostic medical devices
represents class D for in vitro diagnostic medical devices
represents risk class III for medical devices
Classification of the nature or source of a risk associated with the use or failure of a medical device.
Active implantable medical device intended to be introduced into the human body by surgical intervention and to remain in place after the procedure.
Represents the Class IIa risk classification for medical devices.
Represents a corrective action categorized as a recall.
Represents a critical severity level for a medical device risk
represents risk class IIb for medical devices
Medical device intended to be used once only for a single patient and then discarded.
Type of corrective or preventive action taken by the manufacturer in response to a medical device incident or risk.
Represents a low probability level for a medical device risk.

comment.undefined: Estimate of the likelihood of occurrence of harm associated with a hazard or risk in the context of medical device risk management.

comment.en: Represents a moderate severity level for a medical device risk
A missed, delayed or incorrect diagnostic conclusion or communication that represents a missed opportunity for timely and accurate diagnosis

comment.undefined: Medical device intended to be used in vitro for the examination of specimens derived from the human body to provide information for diagnostic, monitoring or compatibility purposes (IVDR).

comment.en: Communication issued by the manufacturer to inform users, distributors or competent authorities about a potential risk associated with a medical device.
Represents a medium probability level for a medical device risk.
Risk related to software failures, algorithms, or cybersecurity issues in software-based medical devices.
Represents a material risk for a risk type
Failure or malfunction of a medical device that prevents it from performing its intended function as specified by the manufacturer
Degree to which a product (device) conforms to specified design, manufacturing and regulatory requirements affecting safety, performance and traceability
Represents a radiation risk for a risk Type

comment.undefined: Incident that directly or indirectly led, might have led or might lead to the death of a patient, user or other person, or to a serious deterioration in their state of health, or to a serious public health threat.

comment.en: Any incident that meets those conditions will be automatically inferred to be a HighRiskIncident
Classification of a medical device according to its inherent risk level as defined in Annex VIII of the MDR or Annex VIII of the IVDR.
Risk arising when a medical device fails to perform its intended function according to the manufacturer's specifications.
represents the In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Device Regulation applicable in the European Union
Represents a open status for a corrective action
Represents a inference risk for a risk Type
Incident that did not result in actual harm but had the potential to cause serious harm under slightly different circumstances.
Action taken by the manufacturer to eliminate the cause of a detected nonconformity or other undesirable situation related to a medical device, including field safety corrective actions.
Represents a  status for a corrective action
Represents a diagnostic  risk for a risk Type
represents the risk category applicable in the European Union
Represents a serious severity level for a medical device risk
Represents an in situ correction corrective action type.
Medical device that uses a source of energy other than that generated by the human body or by gravity to perform its intended function.
Combination of the probability of occurrence of harm and the severity of that harm. This concept is central to the risk management process required for medical devices under the MDR and IVDR.

comment.undefined: Measure of the possible consequences of a hazard or risk on the health of patients, users or other persons.

comment.en: Products or equipment intended for a medical purpose. In the EU, they must undergo a conformity assessment to demonstrate they meet legal requirements to ensure they are safe and perform as intended.
Risk of physical injury to a patient, user or other person resulting from the use or malfunction of a medical device.
Physical or psychological injury, illness, or death experienced by a patient as a result of healthcare or device use, including harms caused or contributed to by device malfunction or misuse
Represents a closed status for a corrective action
Represents a contamination risk for a risk Type
Medical device intended to be used by lay persons for self-testing outside a professional healthcare environment.
Represents the type of incident associated with a medical device.
Measured behaviour or functional characteristics of a device under normal operating conditions, relative to its intended performance specifications

Any malfunction or deterioration in the characteristics or performance of a device, or any inadequacy in the information supplied by the manufacturer, or any undesirable side-effect, that has or could have led to harm to a patient, user or other person.
Any device that meets those conditions will be automatically inferred to be a HighRiskImplantableDevice
Represents a infection risk for a risk Type
Represents a user notice corrective action type.
Medical device intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body by surgical intervention and intended to remain in place after the procedure.
The applicable European Union legal framework for medical devices, primarily Regulation (EU) 2017/745 (MDR) or Regulation (EU) 2017/746 (IVDR).
